REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 14TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AND TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE CITY EMPLOYEES ARE NOT SUBJECT TO IMMUNITY. DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS CITE NO AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER/PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS LIMITED TO CIVIL HARASSMENT CASES DESCRIBED IN THE MOVING PAPERS ("CONFIDENTIAL CLETS FORMS, PETITION FOR TRO"). DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1,5 AND 7-13 IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT CHALLENGES THE VERACITY OF PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS REGARDING CLAIMS SUBMISSION AND ASKS THE COURT TO CONSIDER EVIDENCE PRESENTED IN A DECLARATION ON A DEMURRER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT RYAN PATTERSON'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. This action arose out of a disputed tenancy of a piece of property owned by plaintiff. Initially, plaintiff representing herself sued a host of defendants who she alleged had failed in their legal and contractual duties to assist her in evicting tenants and regaining control over the property. Those defendants included the Mayor, the Sheriff, PG&E, Recology and, at one point, the attorney who was retained to represent one of the property-related defendants. The attorney defendant replied with a SLAPP motion which was granted in the trial court and affirmed on appeal. The successful attorney defendant was awarded fees of $28,937.50 against plaintiff. Meanwhile, in June of this year, the entire action was dismissed by the court without prejudice for plaintiff's failure to appear. While all this was going on, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 708.020, the defendant attorney served interrogatories on plaintiff seeking financial information that would assist him in collecting his fee award. When those interrogatories went unanswered, a motion for an order compelling responses was filed, seeking an additional fee award of $1,950. As with the interrogatories, plaintiff did not respond to the motion to compel. Applying holdings such as those in Maria P. v. Riles, 43 Cal.3d 1281, 1290 (1987) [court retains ancillary jurisdiction to award fees after dismissal for failure to prosecute] to this Court's June, 2024 dismissal, the Court retains ancillary jurisdiction to adjudicate this motion to compel. Accordingly, for good cause shown, the attorney defendant's motion for an order compelling plaintiff to respond to the subject interrogatories is GRANTED. The moving party is also awarded fees in the amount of $1950.00.

Counsel for the moving party shall prepare a proposed order consistent with the foregoing and forward it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)
